element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundation weapon throughout all stages; can swap between two equipped guns
core movement and combat loop established immediately
introduces alternate shot types: homing missiles, torpedoes, flamethrower, laser, spread shot
resource management; using them depletes limited supply
defensive power-up; dropped from flying pods
teaches pattern recognition before final boss encounters
spatial awareness required; touching enemies or bullets also causes life loss
risk-reward move; both weapons at risk if hit during execution
2 of 6 stages feature this perspective; navigation with screen rotation required
shifts from run-and-gun to navigation puzzle; find and destroy predetermined targets
Japanese version: appears on Normal Mode; international versions: Hard Mode only

typical_relief_triggers: reaching safe zones between enemy clusters
typical_tension_triggers: loss of equipped power-up upon death
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: cinematic escalation with stage-to-stage intensity variation; director Nobuya Nakazato designed tighter stage progression than predecessors; music changes throughout stages to emphasize cinematic low-budget-movie atmosphere

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 introduces basic run-gun-jump loop with early power-up availability; manageable enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-4 introduce overhead perspective sections and mid-stage bosses; pit hazards increase; enemy patterns become more complex
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 5-6 feature sustained enemy fire, difficult boss patterns, and resource management pressure; final boss only accessible on Hard Mode (international) or Normal Mode (Japanese)
notable_spikes: first mid-stage boss encounter (teaches pattern memorization)
first overhead perspective stage (mechanic shift; screen rotation required)
final boss fight (true completion challenge; different difficulty gating between regions)

notable_level_patterns.description: 4 of 6 stages use traditional side-scrolling perspective; linear left-to-right advancement with enemy clusters, power-up drops from flying pods, mid-stage and final bosses; spinning jump available as risk-reward evasion tactic

2 of 6 stages use Mode 7 overhead perspective; player navigates to locate and destroy predetermined target objects; screen can be rotated for navigation clarity; different control scheme than side-scrolling stages

All stages feature at least one boss (end-of-stage); many stages include mid-stage bosses; pattern recognition and weapon-type adaptation required; teaches player to cycle between equipped weapon types

Acquiring power-ups replaces current gun's shot type; losing a life immediately strips the power-up from equipped gun; dual-gun spinning jump risks both weapons simultaneously; creates tension between offensive upgrades and defensive vulnerability
